_meta.notes: All monetary values in GBP. All rates as decimals. Never hardcode these values in UI components — always import from this file via the TAX object in fq-calculator.js.

_disclaimer: These rules are verified at the date shown above. Tax rules change at each Budget and tax year. Finio updates this file after each change following professional verification. Always verify planning decisions with a qualified tax adviser before acting.

income.personalAllowanceTaperNote: PA reduces by £1 for every £2 of income above £100,000. Effective 60% marginal rate in this band.
income.scottishNote: Scottish rates apply to Scottish taxpayers on non-savings, non-dividend income only
income.startingRateForSavingsNote: 0% on savings income if non-savings income below £17,570
income.marriageAllowanceNote: Lower earner transfers up to £1,260 of personal allowance to spouse/civil partner
income.highIncomeChildBenefitNote: Child benefit clawed back above £60,000 — 1% per £200 of income above threshold

capitalGains.badrNote: Business Asset Disposal Relief — qualifying business disposals. Rate 18% from 6 April 2026 (was 14%).
capitalGains.investorsReliefNote: Qualifying unlisted shares held at least 3 years
capitalGains.propertyReportingNote: UK residential property gains must be reported and tax paid within 60 days of completion
capitalGains.bedAndISANote: Sell investments and rebuy inside ISA — crystallises gain within annual exempt amount
capitalGains.bedAndSpouseNote: Sell investments and spouse rebuys — resets base cost using their annual exempt amount
capitalGains.thirtyDayRuleNote: Cannot repurchase same shares within 30 days — use different fund or Bed and ISA/Spouse
capitalGains.cryptoNote: HMRC treats cryptoassets as capital assets — each disposal is a CGT event

inheritanceTax.residenceNilRateBandNote: RNRB tapers by £1 for every £2 of estate above £2m
inheritanceTax.ihtReducedRateNote: 36% rate applies if 10% or more of net estate left to qualifying charity
inheritanceTax.pensionIHTInclusionNote: Defined contribution pensions (SIPP, workplace DC, personal pension) become subject to IHT from this date
inheritanceTax.definedBenefitDeathBenefitNote: DB pension lump sum death benefits — usually discretionary trust, mostly outside estate. Complex — adviser needed.
inheritanceTax.lifeInsuranceInTrustNote: Life insurance written in trust is outside the estate. Not in trust = inside estate, subject to IHT.
inheritanceTax.bprQualifyingNote: 100% relief on qualifying business assets, trading company shares, certain AIM shares. 50% on assets used in a business.
inheritanceTax.aprBprCombinedAllowanceNote: Combined APR/BPR allowance £2.5m per individual (transferable to spouse). 100% relief within allowance. 50% above. Refreshes every 7 years for lifetime gifting, 10 years in trusts.
inheritanceTax.aimBPRNote: AIM-listed and not-listed shares: 50% relief only from 6 April 2026 (was 100%). Regardless of allowance position.
inheritanceTax.smallGiftExemptionNote: £250 per recipient per year — cannot combine with annual exemption for same recipient
inheritanceTax.weddingGiftNote: Must be made before the ceremony
inheritanceTax.normalExpenditureExemptionNote: Gifts from surplus income — regular pattern, does not reduce standard of living. Needs documentation.
inheritanceTax.petNote: Potentially exempt transfers — gifts to individuals. Outside estate if donor survives 7 years.
inheritanceTax.cltNote: Chargeable lifetime transfers — gifts into discretionary trusts. 20% entry charge on amounts above NRB.

taperRelief.note: Taper reduces IHT rate on CLTs and failed PETs. Applied to tax due, not to asset value.

inheritanceTax.deedOfVariationNote: Beneficiary can redirect inherited assets within 2 years of death — treated as if deceased made the gift
inheritanceTax.quickSuccessionReliefNote: Reduces IHT if property was inherited and beneficiary dies within 5 years
inheritanceTax.nonDomIHTNote: From April 2025: 10-year UK residence test replaces 17-year deemed domicile rule for IHT purposes
inheritanceTax.excludedPropertyTrustNote: Non-dom assets in qualifying offshore trust before becoming deemed dom — protected under pre-April 2025 rules

pension.taperedAnnualAllowanceNote: Adjusted income over £260,000: AA reduces by £1 for every £2 over threshold. Minimum £10,000.
pension.mpaaNote: Triggered by accessing flexible drawdown income. Kills carry forward for money purchase.
pension.carryForwardNote: Must have been a member of a registered pension scheme in the relevant year
pension.lumpSumAllowanceNote: Maximum tax-free cash across all registered pensions in lifetime. Post-LTA abolition.
pension.taxFreeCashNote: 25% of crystallised fund, up to Lump Sum Allowance
pension.statePensionNote: Full new State Pension 2026/27. Requires 35 qualifying NI years.
pension.statePensionDeferralNote: Deferring State Pension increases it by 1% per 9 weeks (approx 5.8% per year)
pension.higherRateTaxReliefNote: Higher rate taxpayers claim additional 20% via self-assessment or PAYE adjustment
pension.employerContributionsNote: Count toward annual allowance. CT-deductible. No NI.

salarysacrifice.note: Both employer and employee save NI on sacrificed salary. Most tax-efficient route for pension contributions.

pension.annuityNote: Secured income for life. Taxed as pension income. Irreversible.
pension.flexibleDrawdownNote: Income taken flexibly from defined contribution pension. Taxed as income. MPAA triggered on first flexible income payment.
pension.ufplsNote: Uncrystallised Funds Pension Lump Sum: 25% tax-free, 75% taxed as income. Each payment partially tax-free.

deathBefore75.note: Death benefits before age 75 paid tax-free to nominated beneficiary (within LSA limits)

deathAfter75.note: Death benefits after age 75 taxed as income at recipient's marginal rate

pension.nominationNote: Nominations are discretionary — pension trustees decide but usually follow nomination. Review regularly.
pension.definedBenefitNote: Annual allowance test uses pension input amount = 16 × (pension increase) + any lump sum increase
pension.qropsNote: Qualifying Recognised Overseas Pension Scheme — complex rules, adviser essential

isa.lifetimeISAPenaltyNote: 25% withdrawal penalty applies before age 60 except first home purchase or terminal illness
isa.lifetimeISAFirstHomeBuyerNote: Can use for first home purchase up to £450,000
isa.helpToBuyNote: Closed to new applicants. Existing accounts can continue and claim bonus until November 2029.
isa.innovativeFinanceISANote: P2P lending returns tax-free within ISA wrapper
isa.apsNote: Additional Permitted Subscription — surviving spouse inherits deceased's ISA allowance as one-off additional subscription
isa.flexibilityNote: Flexible ISAs allow withdrawals and redeposits within same tax year without using additional allowance
isa.bedAndISANote: Sell investment outside ISA, rebuy inside ISA. Crystallises gain within annual exempt CGT amount.
isa.isaIHTNote: ISA is included in IHT estate unless left to spouse/civil partner

vct.note: Venture Capital Trust. High risk — early-stage company investment.

eis.note: Enterprise Investment Scheme. High risk. IHT exemption after 2 years via BPR.

seis.note: Seed EIS. Highest relief rate. Very early-stage, very high risk.

firstTimeBuyerRelief.note: First-time buyers: 0% up to £425,000, 5% to £625,000. No relief above £625,000.

annualTaxEnvelopedDwellings.note: ATED applies to residential properties worth over £500,000 held by companies

rentalIncome.section24Note: Mortgage interest not deductible from rental income. Tax credit of 20% instead. Higher rate landlords significantly impacted.
rentalIncome.wearAndTearAllowanceNote: Replaced by replacement of domestic items relief — actual cost of replacing items only

cgtOnProperty.lettingRelief: Restricted from April 2020 — only where owner shares occupancy
cgtOnProperty.overseasPropertyNote: UK residents pay UK CGT on all worldwide property gains. Overseas tax credit may apply.

furnishedHolidayLettings.note: FHL special tax status removed April 2024. Now taxed as ordinary residential rental income.

nationalInsurance.class3Note: Voluntary contributions to fill State Pension gaps. Compare cost vs benefit before paying.
nationalInsurance.employmentAllowanceNote: Eligible employers can reduce employer NI bill by up to £5,000/year
nationalInsurance.directorNIMethod: Annual earnings period — different from monthly employee calculation

discretionaryTrust.entryChargeNote: 20% on CLTs above NRB. Effective 20% — half the death rate.

periodicCharge.note: 6% of value above NRB at 10-year anniversary

exitCharge.note: Proportionate charge based on time elapsed since last 10-year anniversary

bareTrust.note: Assets taxed as if beneficiary owns them — income and gains attributed to beneficiary

interestInPossession.note: Income to life tenant, taxed as their income. Assets form part of their estate for IHT.

trustRegistrationService.note: Most trusts must register with HMRC TRS. Penalties for non-compliance.

statutoryResidenceTest.note: Automatic UK tests and sufficient ties test determine UK tax residence. Complex — professional advice essential.

nonDom.newArrivalNote: New UK residents exempt from UK tax on foreign income and gains for first 4 years
nonDom.ihtNote: 10 years UK residence triggers worldwide IHT exposure under new rules
nonDom.oldRegimeNote: Pre-April 2025 deemed domicile rules still relevant for existing arrangements

remittanceBasis.note: Available under old rules for non-doms. Taxed only on income/gains remitted to UK. Annual charge for long-term residents. Replaced by new regime from April 2025 for new claims.

overseasWorkdayRelief.note: Inbound assignees may exempt proportion of employment income attributable to overseas workdays. Professional advice needed.

doubleTaxationRelief.creditMethod: Offset overseas tax paid against UK liability — most common
doubleTaxationRelief.exemptionMethod: Some treaty income exempt from UK tax entirely
doubleTaxationRelief.note: UK has double taxation treaties with over 130 countries. Treatment varies. User declares jurisdiction — Finio shows UK tax treatment only.

splitYearTreatment.note: Year of arrival or departure may be split — part UK resident, part not. Complex rules.

foreignCurrencyGains.note: Gains on foreign currency bank accounts taxable as CGT above personal use. Personal use exemption for travel money.

cryptoOverseas.note: Overseas crypto exchanges — HMRC still expects UK tax reporting on all gains

salaryVsDividend.note: Optimal split between salary (NI cost, CT deductible) and dividends (no NI, lower tax rates). Pension contributions change the calculation significantly.

employerPensionContributions.note: Employer contributions count toward annual allowance. Most tax-efficient extraction route.

relevantLifePlan.note: Death-in-service via company. Not a benefit in kind. CT-deductible. Outside employee's estate if in trust.

businessOwnerPersonal.badrNote: Business Asset Disposal Relief on qualifying share or business sale. Rate 18% from 6 April 2026.
businessOwnerPersonal.bprQualifying: 100% IHT relief within £2.5m combined APR/BPR allowance after 2 years. 50% above allowance.
businessOwnerPersonal.aimBPRNote: AIM shares 50% relief from April 2026 regardless of allowance position (was 100%).

employeeOwnershipTrust.note: Sale of controlling interest to EOT is exempt from CGT. Employees get annual bonus up to £3,600 tax-free.

ir35.note: Off-payroll working rules — if caught by IR35, income treated as employment income. NI and income tax apply.

directorsLoan.s455Note: Outstanding director's loan at year end — company pays S455 tax. Repayable if loan repaid within 9 months.

corporationTax.note: Corporation tax filing and compliance is out of scope for Finio. Personal tax impact of business ownership is in scope.
corporationTax.marginalReliefNote: Marginal relief between £50,000 and £250,000 profits — effective rate tapers between 19% and 25%

makingTaxDigital.note: MTD for income tax — mandatory quarterly reporting for self-employed and landlords above threshold. Phased implementation.

selfAssessment.paymentOnAccountNote: Self-employed and landlords may have to make payments on account in January and July

bengenNote: 4% safe withdrawal rate (Bengen rule). Investable assets × 4% = safe annual spending ceiling.

milestones._note: Timeline milestone catalogue v1.0 (UK-2026.1). Starter set — pending founder review per TL v1.0 §10.3. Each entry: id · category · target object · label.

_nextReviewDue: October 2026 (Autumn Budget) and April 2027 (new tax year)
